summary: Ukrainian forces struck the chemical plant in Novomoskovsk, the fuel depot in Ribinsk and the Krimsky Titan plant, causing fires and production halts. Russian air defenses reported shooting down 91 Shahed and other UAVs across the north, south and east. Intensive UAV activity continued over multiple Ukrainian regions, with several attacks reported on cities and infrastructure.
key_points: Ukrainian strikes hit the chemical plant "Azot" in Novomoskovsk, Tula region, and a fuel depot in Yaroslavl region, causing fires and damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81906).
Russian air defenses reported downing 91 hostile UAVs of various types, including Shahed, Gerbera and Ittilmas, across the north, south and east of Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
Ukrainian forces attacked the Krimsky Titan plant in Armenskoye, halting production of titanium products, rocket fuel, powder and explosives (Source: @UkraineNow/81896).
Three 250‑kg FAB‑250 bombs were dropped on Sloviansk, injuring at least five people including a child and damaging over 20 apartment buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81898).
Russian UAV fragments fell on industrial facilities in Novomoskovsk, Tula region (Source: @tass_agency/380557).
Ukrainian air defense shot down four UAVs heading towards Moscow (Source: @tass_agency/380564).
Ukrainian sanctions were imposed on ten Russian mobile operators and internet providers serving occupied territories (Source: @UkraineNow/81895).
Multiple UAV sightings reported across Ukrainian oblasts, including Shahed attacks on Mykolaiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and the Black Sea coast (Various sources).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces struck key Russian‑linked industrial sites, including the Azot chemical plant in Novomoskovsk and the Ribinsk fuel depot, causing fires and halting production. Russian air defenses claimed to have downed 91 hostile UAVs of various types across Ukraine, reflecting intense aerial contest. UAV activity remains high, with numerous sightings and attacks reported over multiple regions.

points: Ukrainian forces attacked the chemical plant "Azot" in Novomoskovsk, Tula region, and a fuel depot in Yaroslavl region, resulting in fires and damage to state fuel reserves (Source: @UkraineNow/81906).
The Krimsky Titan plant in Armenskoye was hit, igniting a fire and halting production of titanium products, rocket fuel, powder and explosives (Source: @UkraineNow/81896).
Ukrainian sanctions targeted ten Russian mobile operators and internet providers that support occupied territories, signed by the President (Source: @UkraineNow/81895).
Ukrainian air defense reported shooting down four UAVs en route to Moscow (Source: @tass_agency/380564).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed UAV attack on Mykolaiv from the south (Source: @kpszsu/65141).
Multiple UAV sightings were reported across Mykolaiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and the Black Sea coast, indicating widespread aerial activity (Various sources).
Ukrainian forces claimed a UAV strike on the Ribinsk fuel depot, a strategic storage complex 270 km north of Moscow, with video evidence of a burning reservoir (Source: @eRadarrua/96485).

Russian air defenses reported destroying or suppressing 91 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, Ittilmas and imitation drones, across northern, southern and eastern Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
Fragments of downed UAVs fell on the industrial enterprise in Novomoskovsk, Tula region (Source: @tass_agency/380557).
Russian authorities announced that 249 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down over Russian regions (Source: @tass_agency/380570).
A fire in a private house in Smolensk region was caused by a UAV attack, injuring an elderly woman (Source: @tass_agency/380568).
Ukrainian UAV fragments impacted fuel storage facilities in Yaroslavl region (Source: @tass_agency/380565).
Russian air traffic was disrupted with road closures near Yaroslavl due to a UAV attack (Source: @tass_agency/380556).
Russian forces dropped three FAB‑250 bombs on Sloviansk, causing civilian casualties and extensive building damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81898).
UAVs were reported heading towards Sumy from the north and northwest, and towards Kharkiv regions such as Bogoduhiv and Luhansk direction (Various sources).
A Shahed UAV was observed over Mykolaiv from the south (Source: @kpszsu/65141).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on the Ribinsk fuel depot, a strategic complex north of Moscow (Source: @eRadarrua/96485).
Russian air defenses claimed 91 UAVs downed across the east, including attacks from Orël, Bryansk and Primorsko-Akhtarsk directions (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
UAV activity was noted over Zaporizhzhia from the southwest and over the Black Sea coast targeting Chornomorsk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67592).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV attack on the Krimsky Titan plant in Armenskoye, affecting production (Source: @UkraineNow/81896).
Multiple UAV sightings over Kharkiv oblast, including Bogoduhiv and Luhansk direction (Source: @kpszsu/65148).
UAVs were reported over Sumy from the north and northwest, including a direction towards Lebedyn and Krivelets (Various sources).
Russian air defenses reported downing UAVs over northern Ukraine as part of the 91‑UAV tally (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
UAV fragments fell on industrial sites in Novomoskovsk, Tula region, indicating northern Russian territory impact (Source: @tass_agency/380557).
Ukrainian radar noted UAV activity over Chernihiv region, including a new reactive UAV from Bryansk heading to Snovsk (Source: @eRadarrua/96437).
UAV sightings over Kharkiv's Lozniv district moving towards Pavlohrad in Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @kpszsu/65132).
UAVs reported near the airport in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @eRadarrua/96483).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed UAV over Mykolaiv from the south (Source: @kpszsu/65141).
UAVs were reported over Zaporizhzhia from the southwest and over the Black Sea coast targeting Chornomorsk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67592).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed UAV over Mykolaiv from the south (Source: @kpszsu/65141).
UAV activity noted over Odesa region, including attacks near Liman and over the sea (Various sources).
Russian air defenses reported downing UAVs across southern Ukraine as part of the 91‑UAV count (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
UAV fragments impacted fuel storage facilities in Yaroslavl region, affecting southern Russian logistics (Source: @tass_agency/380565).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on the Krimsky Titan plant in Armenskoye, a southern Russian‑linked facility (Source: @UkraineNow/81896).
UAV sightings over Kherson region reported power outage due to an accident (Source: @tass_agency/380570).
Russian air defenses claimed to have destroyed 91 hostile UAVs of various types across Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
Four UAVs heading towards Moscow were shot down by Russian air defenses (Source: @tass_agency/380564).
Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV attacks on industrial targets in Russia, including Novomoskovsk and Ribinsk (Various sources).
UAV fragments fell on industrial enterprises in Novomoskovsk, Tula region (Source: @tass_agency/380557).
Ukrainian air defense reported downing UAVs over the northern, southern and eastern fronts as part of the 91‑UAV tally (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
Russian authorities reported 249 Ukrainian UAVs shot down over Russian territory (Source: @tass_agency/380570).
UAV activity continued over the Black Sea coast, with attacks reported on Chornomorsk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67592).

key_developments: Ukrainian strikes ignited fires at the Azot chemical plant in Novomoskovsk and a fuel depot in Yaroslavl, disrupting Russian fuel reserves (Source: @UkraineNow/81906).
Russian air defenses reported downing 91 hostile UAVs, including Shahed and Gerbera types, across multiple fronts (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
Three FAB‑250 bombs were dropped on Sloviansk, causing civilian injuries and extensive building damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81898).
Ukrainian sanctions targeted ten Russian mobile operators and internet providers supporting occupied territories (Source: @UkraineNow/81895).
Ukrainian forces hit the Krimsky Titan plant, halting production of titanium and explosive materials (Source: @UkraineNow/81896).
Russian authorities reported 249 Ukrainian UAVs shot down over Russian regions (Source: @tass_agency/380570).
UAV fragments fell on industrial facilities in Novomoskovsk, Tula region, indicating cross‑border UAV incursions (Source: @tass_agency/380557).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV strikes on Russian industrial sites demonstrate an expanding capability to target logistics and production hubs deep within occupied territory, raising the risk of further infrastructure degradation. Russian air defenses claim high interception rates, yet continued UAV penetrations suggest gaps in coverage, especially over southern and eastern approaches. The deployment of heavy FAB‑250 bombs by Russian forces in Sloviansk indicates an escalation in munition use against civilian areas, increasing humanitarian risk.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have imposed sanctions on Russian telecom operators, aiming to disrupt communications for occupied administrations. Russian regional governors report extensive UAV debris and fires, prompting emergency responses and temporary road closures. International observers note the high volume of UAV activity as a sign of intensified aerial contest, potentially influencing diplomatic discussions on escalation control.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source UAV sightings across multiple oblasts reveal coordinated attack patterns from north, south and east, suggesting a broad operational tempo by Ukrainian forces. The reported downing of 91 UAVs includes a mix of Shahed, Gerbera and imitation drones, indicating diverse threat vectors. Russian claims of 249 UAVs shot down over its territory may reflect heightened defensive posturing but require independent verification.
